title: vicia vicina var. pallidiflora
intro: Vicia vicina var. pallidiflora, also known as pale vetch, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Vicia vicina var. pallidiflora include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and diuretic properties, which can help to reduce pain, ease muscle spasms, and promote urination. Herbal preparations of Vicia vicina var. pallidiflora can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
